free_text: Experimental fragmentation studies with C-12 therapy
Charged particles (H, He, Li, Be, B)
- Entrance and exit windows each 2mm PMMA.
of all material in the beam path.
Fragment-charge identification by DE and TOF
The data correspond to those shown in E.Haettners
Master thesis,with the following small modifications:
- as described by E.Haettner (p.77) in some cases the
fragment data were screened by primary beam events,
in particular those taken at small angles(0-2 deg).
The corresponding data are marked by shaded areas
in the figures. Therefore, for d=15.9cm the data
for 0-2 deg. were omitted and for d=25.8cm the data
with energies from 70-100 MeV/u were left out.
- I have chosen a lower limit of 40 MeV/u in most
- for the conversion from MeV to MeV/u the
simplifying assumption was made that only the most
abundant isotope is produced for each element Z
One hydrogen point (28.8 g/cm2) could not be
completely detected by the thin scintillator.
